title: taudit Authority Graph
description: Standalone, versioned authority-graph export from `taudit graph --format json`. Models how authority (secrets, identities) propagates through a CI/CD pipeline. Stable contract — additive changes ship under 1.x.y; breaking changes ship under 2.0.0. The $id is a logical identifier; the URL may not resolve. Treat as schema namespace, not as a content fetch endpoint.

schema_version.description: Semver of this schema. 1.x.y additions are non-breaking; 2.0.0 means breaking changes.

schema_uri.description: Canonical URI for the schema this document conforms to.

graph.description: The authority graph for a single pipeline file.

AuthorityGraph.description: Directed authority graph. Nodes are pipeline elements (steps, secrets, artifacts, identities, images). Edges model authority/data flow.

source.description: Where the pipeline definition came from.

nodes.description: All nodes in the graph. Node ids are dense integers (0..nodes.length).

edges.description: All directed edges in the graph. Edge ids are dense integers (0..edges.length).

completeness.description: How complete is this graph? Set by the parser.

completeness_gaps.description: Human-readable reasons why the graph is partial (omitted when empty).

completeness_gap_kinds.description: Typed categories for each completeness gap, parallel to completeness_gaps (omitted when empty).

metadata.description: Graph-level metadata set by parsers (e.g. trigger type, platform-specific flags). Omitted when empty.

parameters.description: Top-level pipeline parameters (ADO) used for parameter-interpolation analysis. Keys are parameter names.

param_type.description: Declared parameter type (string, number, boolean, object, etc.). Empty when omitted (defaults to string).

has_values_allowlist.description: True when the parameter declares a values: allowlist that constrains acceptable inputs.

PipelineSource.description: The on-disk (or in-VCS) location the graph was built from.

file.description: Path to the pipeline file as taudit observed it.

repo.description: Optional repository URL or slug, when known.

git_ref.description: Optional git ref (branch, tag, or SHA) the file was read from.

commit_sha.description: SHA of the commit being analyzed; reproducibility hint when set.

AuthorityCompleteness.description: How completely the parser was able to model authority relationships.

NodeKind.description: Semantic kind of a graph node. step = a runnable instruction; secret = a sensitive value; identity = a token or principal; image = an action/container reference; artifact = data crossing step boundaries.

TrustZone.description: Explicit trust classification on every node. first_party = code/config you own; third_party = pinned external action or image; untrusted = unpinned action, fork PR input, or otherwise unverified source.

EdgeKind.description: Edge semantics model authority/data flow, not syntactic YAML relations. has_access_to = step reads a secret/identity at runtime; produces = step writes an artifact; consumes = artifact flows into a step; uses_image = step delegates execution to an image/action; delegates_to = step hands control to another step (cross-job or composite); persists_to = step writes a credential to disk, outliving the step's lifetime.

Node.description: A node in the authority graph.

id.description: Dense integer id; matches the index of this node in graph.nodes.

name.description: Display name (step name, secret name, action ref, etc.).

metadata.description: Flexible string-keyed metadata: pinning status, digest, identity scope, permissions, container/oidc/self-hosted markers, parent job_name, etc. Keys are documented in crates/taudit-core/src/graph.rs (META_* constants).

Edge.description: A directed edge in the authority graph.

id.description: Dense integer id; matches the index of this edge in graph.edges.

authority_summary.description: Optional stamped summary on has_access_to edges to identity nodes (ADR 0002 Phase 2). Omitted when absent.

AuthorityEdgeSummary.description: Allowlisted copy of authority-relevant fields from the target identity for a has_access_to edge. Not a generic metadata bag.

identity_scope.description: Copy of identity_scope metadata when present.

permissions_summary.description: Truncated copy of permissions metadata when present.
